title: Vite project and dependency pinning

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Vite project and dependency pinning; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Vite project and dependency pinning

canonicalExplanation.summary: A React project needs pinned React/build versions, one understood entry path, module boundaries, tests, and production build behavior.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A Vite project renders under StrictMode, keeps browser-safe config explicit, and verifies a production build. Your goal is to create and reproduce a React project from its manifest and lockfile. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: javascript project browser app; Application root and rendering; git tools dependencies. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A React project needs pinned React/build versions, one understood entry path, module boundaries, tests, and production build behavior. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Development Strict Mode can repeat work, environment values can be public, and framework conventions differ. This draft therefore rejects: If the development server works, dependency and production configuration are necessarily correct.

includes: Run development, build and preview commands while preserving a reviewed lockfile.
Create and reproduce a React project from its manifest and lockfile.
excludes: Development Strict Mode can repeat work, environment values can be public, and framework conventions differ.

outcomes: Create and reproduce a React project from its manifest and lockfile.

examples.title: Vite project and dependency pinning: concrete case
examples.situation: A Vite project renders under StrictMode, keeps browser-safe config explicit, and verifies a production build.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A Vite project renders under StrictMode, keeps browser-safe config explicit, and verifies a production build.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Verify the pinned production build and runtime configuration; development success is not deployment evidence.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: If the development server works, dependency and production configuration are necessarily correct.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete React: Component-Based Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: If the development server works, dependency and production configuration are necessarily correct.

analogy.example: A state-driven component workshop can model Vite project and dependency pinning, where inputs and state produce a UI description.

limitations: A workshop does not reproduce reconciliation identity, hook ordering, concurrent rendering, effects, or browser accessibility APIs.
